reason: The arithmetic absorption eta<=sqrt(eta) on [0,eta_0] for eta_0<=1 (the genuine content of 1.7) is CORRECT and the defect-absorption into eps=C sqrt(eta) with C=max(C_1..C_4) is correct. But the final parenthetical 'take eta_0=1/4' is mathematically INADMISSIBLE and breaks the binding constraint. GT-Pprops (lem-P-properties, validated; registry contract reads 'eta <= eta_0 < 1/4') requires eta_0<1/4 STRICTLY: its export node (line 53) constructs R=(S^2)^{-1/2} from the binomial series f(x)=x^{-1/2} centered at 1, radius rho=1, and explicitly 'FIX eta_0<1/4 so that 4 eta_0<1'. The bounding constant C(eta_0)=sum_{n>=1}|a_n|(4 eta_0)^{n-1}, a_n=C(-1/2,n) with |a_n|=C(2n,n)/4^n ~ 1/sqrt(pi n), is FINITE only for 4 eta_0<1 and DIVERGES at the boundary 4 eta_0=1 (sum_{n>=1}|a_n| ~ 2 sqrt(N/pi); verified numerically: partial sum to N=2e6 is ~1594.8, matching 2 sqrt(2e6/pi)=1595.8). So at eta=eta_0=1/4 the series for P=theta(2Phi-1) does NOT converge: A=Im P is undefined and every imported lemma (GT-Pprops/GT-easy/GT-jordan/GT-orderunit) is inapplicable. eta_0=1/4 sits exactly on the GT-Pprops divergence boundary and contradicts the cited 'eta_0<1/4'. FIX: take any eta_0<1/4 (e.g. eta_0=1/8 or 1/5); then eta_0<1/4<1 satisfies BOTH the strict GT-Pprops radius AND the eta<=1 absorption requirement. Replace 'take eta_0=1/4' with 'take eta_0=1/8 (any value strictly below 1/4)'.
